title: Switchable chiral transport in charge-ordered kagome metal CsV3Sb5
type1_summary: No clearly replot-ready, figure-organized tabular dataset was identifiable from the provided file report.
type2_summary: One source-data PDF is present and is conservatively treated as raw/underlying supporting material rather than cleaned figure-ready data.
classification_notes: The classification is conservative because only one file was reported and its internal contents were not inspected. If the PDF contains figure-labeled tables, it could potentially be Type 1, but that is not evidenced here.

file_classifications.paper_evidence: The paper states that data supporting the findings are deposited to Zenodo and the Methods/availability text indicates raw supporting data are included. For this paper, the source-data deposit is expected to contain underlying measurement outputs rather than only figure-ready tables.
file_classifications.file_evidence: Single 2.3 MB PDF in a nature_source_data folder. A PDF source-data supplement of this size is more consistent with raw/underlying supporting material or a compiled source-data package than with a simple replot-ready table file. No evidence here of tabular headers or figure-specific structure was provided.
file_classifications.reasoning: Given the paper's explicit data-deposit language and the lack of any file-level evidence for a clean figure-specific table, the conservative classification is raw/underlying supporting material rather than Type 1. The file is not directly confirmed to be replot-ready, and the PDF format suggests documentation or compiled source data rather than a simple cleaned dataset.
file_classifications.ambiguity: Moderate. The file is a PDF and could contain source-data tables or supplementary documentation, but no internal structure was provided. Without table headers or figure labels, Type 1 cannot be justified.
file_classifications.key_columns_or_structure: Not inspectable from the provided report; likely supplementary source-data PDF rather than a tabular dataset

paper_analysis.summary: This paper reports switchable electronic magnetochiral anisotropy (eMChA) in the kagome metal CsV3Sb5, measured by second-harmonic transport under an in-plane magnetic field. The key finding is that a strong chiral transport signal emerges only deep inside the charge-ordered phase below about 35 K, and its handedness can be reversed by changing the sign of the out-of-plane field component, linking transport chirality to charge order and time-reversal-symmetry breaking.
measurement_types: STM results from prior work (cited, not newly measured here)
muon-relaxation / Nernst / STM data from prior work (cited for comparison)

figures.description: Schematic of resistance in normal versus chiral conductors, showing the magnetochiral term proportional to B·I.
Illustration of nonlinear I-V behavior and second-harmonic voltage generation in a chiral conductor under AC current.
Cartoon of possible mechanisms for eMChA: chiral crystal structure, chiral scattering centers, or electronic interaction-driven chirality.
Schematic of CsV3Sb5 showing high-temperature mirror-symmetric structure and low-temperature spontaneous symmetry breaking enabling eMChA.
Microfabricated low-strain transport device geometry on membrane springs.
Field dependence of second-harmonic voltage V2ω at low temperature for current along the c axis with magnetic field nearly in-plane.
Temperature dependence of antisymmetrized second-harmonic signal ΔV2ω, compared with 4a0 CDW Fourier intensity and anomalous Nernst effect from prior work.
Angular dependence of V2ω for two current orientations, showing a sharp sign reversal near the kagome-plane orientation.
Angular dependence of V2ω at several temperatures for fixed magnetic field.
Angular dependence of V2ω at several magnetic fields for fixed low temperature.
Field-dependent eMChA coefficient ΔR/(R|B||J|) at various angles, with magnetoresistance inset.
Chiral conductivity Δσ versus magnetic field at various angles.
Angular dependence of the derivative ∂(Δσ)/∂B compared with a theoretical model curve.
Cartoon of chirality reversal near in-plane magnetic field and domain formation.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: The paper reports raw transport measurements on CsV3Sb5 devices: low-frequency AC current-driven voltage signals including first-harmonic Vω and second-harmonic V2ω, field sweeps up to 18 T, temperature sweeps from above TCDW down to low temperature, and angle-dependent magnetotransport with current along out-of-plane and in-plane directions. It also reports X-ray diffraction characterization of crystals. The Methods mention two membrane-based devices and a reference strained device, plus magnetoresistivity and Hall measurements.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The paper states: 'Data that support the findings of this study are deposited to Zenodo with the access link: https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.6787797.' It also says that methods, additional references, Nature Research reporting summaries, source data, extended data, supplementary information, and details of author contributions and competing interests are available online.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw data are not explicitly enumerated as files or formats in the text, but the study clearly collected experimental transport traces (Vω, V2ω, magnetoresistance, angular sweeps, temperature sweeps) and X-ray diffraction patterns. The paper does not specify file formats in the provided text.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include antisymmetrized second-harmonic signals ΔV2ω = V2ω(18 T) - V2ω(-18 T), derived eMChA coefficients ΔR/(R|B||J|), chiral conductivity Δσ, and comparisons to literature datasets such as 4a0 Fourier intensity and anomalous Nernst effect. Theoretical quantities are also derived from transport analysis.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure-specific data are clearly implied by the plotted transport curves and derived quantities in Figs. 2-4. The paper explicitly mentions 'see Methods for full data' and references 'Extended Data' figures, indicating source/figure data were prepared for the plots, but the text does not specify whether separate source-data files are bundled.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No analysis scripts, notebooks, or code repository are mentioned in the provided text. The paper only mentions data deposition to Zenodo and theoretical calculations performed with Quantum ESPRESSO and tight-binding modeling.
dataset_characterization.notes: The dataset is primarily experimental transport data with substantial post-processing: antisymmetrization in magnetic field, extraction of second-harmonic response, conversion to eMChA coefficients, and comparison to theoretical conductivity models. The paper also includes simulation/theory outputs (DFT band structure and tight-binding calculations) but does not describe them as separate downloadable files. The text strongly suggests figure-ready processed data exist, but explicit source-data file naming is not given in the excerpt.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The Methods describe direct measurements of magnetoresistivity, Hall resistivity, and second-harmonic voltage under AC current, plus X-ray diffraction characterization of crystals.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: The paper says 'Data that support the findings of this study are deposited to Zenodo' and refers to 'full data' for the antisymmetrized temperature dependence and to Extended Data figures, implying figure-ready processed datasets.
classification_prior.data_availability_section_relevant: “Data that support the findings of this study are deposited to Zenodo with the access link: https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.6787797.”; “see Methods for full data”; “Extended Data Fig. 1–10”; “source data” is mentioned in the online content list.

inspection_reports.note: PDF file, cannot inspect data content
